figures.caption: Fig. 2. ecDNA copy number heterogeneity is generated rapidly and extends to division time 562 heterogeneity. (a) Schematic of live-cell imaging cell line engineering and image acquisition and 563 analysis pipeline. The engineered live-cell imaging cell lines originating from COLO 320DM and 564 COLO 320HSR were named TG19 ec and TG19 HSR, respectively. (b) Representative images 565 from live-cell imaging capturing a cancer cell as it divides and expands to a population of eight 566 cells in TG19 ec (top) and TG19 HSR (bottom). Scale bars, 20 µm. (c) Quantification of TetR-567 mNeonGreen foci area in each cell in the TG19 ec and TG19 HSR lineages shown in (b). Each 568 node represents a single cell, with connections relating parent cells to their daughter cells in the 569 subsequent generation. TetR-mNeonGreen foci area was used as a proxy for MYC copy number. 570 TetR-mNeonGreen foci area is likely underestimated in the parent generation due to condensed 571 chromatin configuration at the time of image acquisition. (d) Representative images (left) and 572 quantification (right) TetR-mNeonGreen foci area and time to division (T2D) from live-cell imaging 573 and lineage tracking of TG19 ec. P = parent, F1_x = daughter cells (Generation 1), F2_x = 574 granddaughter cells (Generation 2), F3 = great granddaughter cells (Generation 3). Red dashed 575 outlines indicate the most recent division since the last timeframe. Scale bars, 5 µm. (e) 576 Representative images (left) and quantification (right) of TetR-mNeonGreen foci area and T2D 577 from live-cell imaging and lineage tracking of TG19 HSR. P = parent, F1_x = daughter cells 578 (Generation 1), F2_x = granddaughter cells (Generation 2). Scale bars, 5 µm. (f) Quantification 579 of TetR-mNeonGreen foci area difference between sister cells in TG19 ec (n=20 sister cell pairs) 580 and TG19 HSR (n=18 sister cell pairs). Each dot represents the difference between two sister 581
Fig. 3. Dynamic optimal ecDNA copy numbers drive cell adaptation. (a) Density curves 672 depicting copy number distributions in low, middle, high, and unsorted GBM39-EC and GBM39-673

Fig. 1. EcDNA-conferred copy number heterogeneity extends to transcript, protein, and 506 pathway levels. (a) Representative images of DNA FISH for MYC and EGFR in COLO 320 and 507 GBM39 isogenic cell line pairs, respectively (top). Scale bars, 10 µm. Quantification of ecDNA 508 copy number measured by DNA FISH area for COLO 320 and GBM39 isogenic cell line pairs 509 (bottom). (b) Heatmap depicting Pearson correlation coefficients between amplicon copy number 510 and transcript expression from single-cell multiomics sequencing (scATAC-seq and scRNA-seq) 511 in several cell lines. Strength of correlation (R) depicted only for statistically significant correlations 512

Fig. 4. Shift to higher copy number accounts for increased tumourigenicity of ecDNA(+) 702 cells in vivo. (a) Schematic illustrating competition assay workflow with COLO 320DM-mCherry 703 and COLO 320HSR-GFP cells. (b) Change in cell composition, expressed as the percentage of 704 COLO 320DM-mCherry and COLO 320HSR-GFP cells, at the start and end of competition assays 705 performed in culture and in xenograft mouse models. (c) Tumourigenicity plots depicting rate of 706 tumour growth in COLO 320DM (n=5 mice) and COLO 320HSR (n=5 mice) xenograft mouse 707 models. Initial seeding density of 125,000 cells. (d) Mean tumour volume on day 20 in COLO 708
